scenario.context: Station 03 of the day run: plan data structures and use them in Python/Java.
scenario.mission: You choose suitable structures and justify your choice for different usage scenarios.

customerRequest.subject: Station 03: Data structure decisions
customerRequest.body: Please make well-founded data structure decisions for Python and Java components.

options.text: Order does not matter -> list is mandatory
Solve every case with the same structure

feedback.correct: Correct: Access pattern and structure are assigned cleanly.
feedback.wrong: Partly wrong: Please align the structure choice more strictly with the access pattern.

questions.title: Python/Java trade-off
questions.prompt: What is especially important when choosing a language for data structures?

options.text: Only pay attention to syntax preference
Consider libraries, performance, and team competence together

feedback.correct: Correct: The decision has to be justified from several angles.
feedback.wrong: Not suitable: Language choice needs technical and organizational criteria.

questions.title: Build a data-structure decision
questions.prompt: Put the decision process in order.

feedback.correct: Correct: The decision is methodically sound.
feedback.wrong: Not ideal: Please place analysis and comparison before the final documentation.

questions.title: Estimate lookup time
questions.prompt: A structure needs 2 ms per lookup. With 65 lookups, how many ms in total?

feedback.wrong: Please multiply quantity and duration correctly.

questions.title: Justify structure choice
questions.prompt: Formulate in 4-6 sentences a justified choice of data structure for a ticket-status cache.
questions.expectedOutline: Justification of the choice through access pattern, language context, and maintainability.

feedback.correct: Correct: The structure choice is argued in a traceable way.
feedback.wrong: Please connect access, structure, and reasoning more concretely.
